Home › About Us

About Us

Our Story

Why the Foundation Exists

GB United Foundation was created to close a real gap. Too many children in Los Angeles have no access to organized sports, quality coaching, or the sense of belonging that comes from being on a team.

Soccer is the vehicle. The real impact is confidence, discipline, and opportunity for kids who've had too little of all three.

We work in underserved neighborhoods and public schools across LA — bringing coaching, gear, and community to children whose families can't afford club sports.

See Our Programs
👁

Vision

A Los Angeles where no child is left out of sport because of where they live or what their family can afford.

🚀

Mission

To unite and uplift our community by providing equitable access to soccer, fostering confidence, teamwork, and personal development in every child we serve.

🎯

Focus

Underserved children and public schools throughout Los Angeles — building skills alongside inclusion, confidence, and belonging.

Leadership

Full bios and photos coming soon. Our board brings deep expertise, community roots, and a genuine commitment to the kids and families we serve across Los Angeles.

Transparency

Financials

GB United Foundation is committed to full transparency. Financial statements and 990 filings will be made available here as the organization grows.

Our annual report outlines program reach, donor impact, and how every dollar was allocated.

📋 990 Filing

As a registered 501(c)(3), our IRS Form 990 is available to the public upon request.

Request 990 →

💚 Fund Allocation

100% of program funds go directly to coaching, equipment, and access. Admin costs kept minimal.

What We Stand For

Core Values

01

Equity

Access should never depend on income. Every child gets the same shot.

02

Community

We build belonging — every child as part of something bigger than themselves.

03

Excellence

High standards in everything we do, because our kids deserve nothing less.

04

Growth

Skills on the field. Confidence, discipline, and character off it.

Equitable access to soccer for every kid in Los Angeles.
